authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
U.S. Forest Service — Eldorado National Forest (Desolation Wilderness)

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Half Moon Lake not listed)

CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Half Moon Lake not listed; the Tahoe Lake tributary closure §7.50(b)(152) reaches only flowing tributaries upstream to the first lake)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (Half Moon Lake not listed among El Dorado Co. release waters)
Desolation Wilderness Permits — Eldorado National Forest, U.S. Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use. A quota in place from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30 of each year.

parameters.note: Half Moon Lake is a high alpine lake in the Desolation Wilderness (El Dorado Co.), within the Lake Tahoe basin but far upstream of the 'first lake' — it drains via Glen Alpine Creek to Fallen Leaf Lake and then via Taylor Creek to Lake Tahoe — so the special Lake Tahoe tributary closure (§7.50(b)(152), which reaches only flowing tributaries upstream to the first lake) does not apply to the lake itself. Half Moon Lake is not listed by name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation §5.85(a)(1): open all year, 5/10. Species are left empty: Half Moon Lake is not in CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document among El Dorado Co. release waters, and no authoritative CDFW/USFS source names current fish presence; third-party angler reports historically describe a self-sustaining brook trout population, but that is not an authoritative source and is not recorded here.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Half Moon Lake is wholly within California (El Dorado Co.).

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Half Moon Lake lies within the Desolation Wilderness (Eldorado National Forest). A wilderness permit is required year-round for overnight use, and an overnight quota is in place from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30; day-use visitors self-register at trailhead permit stations during the quota season. Access is on foot from the Glen Alpine trailhead (above Lily Lake / Fallen Leaf Lake).

raw.quota_season: Friday before Memorial Day through September 30

regulations.citation: USFS Eldorado National Forest — Desolation Wilderness Permits
regulations.humanSummary: Inside the Desolation Wilderness: a wilderness permit is required year-round for overnight use, with an overnight quota from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30.
regulations.verbatimText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use. A quota in place from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30 of each year.
